marital_status: Married, or in a domestic partnership
employment_status: Employed part time (up to 39 hours per week)
practice_description: My artistic practice seeks to promote a connection with ourselves and the natural world by creating environments using the language of architecture and geology. In response to my alienation from nature, I use excursions to protected lands, research of geological features, as well as studies of ancient architecture and Eastern art, to begin my inquiries. Through drawing and printmaking, I reduce the landscape to specific components in order to encourage focused attention and provide a tool for self reflection.
unique_resources: Second State Press, community print shop has equipment I need to create my artwork, I also teach adult workshops here. My job as an assistant to an Art Consultant exposes me to the world of grant writing and funding non-profits. My job as a Museum Educator gives me access to the Philadelphia Museum of Arts collection and special exhibitions, since I teach with the collection I'm able to develop deeper connections to the artwork.

practice_description:
I practice a lot of different mediums of art that dance around the line between chaos and control. Through tribal baroque imagery I dissect what is in front of us using iconic imagery and emotion to reveal what is beneath the surface. 

I'm trying to find focus in something specific to hopefully find sustainability in a creative practice. I think that is currently leading toward textile design.

marital_status: Married, or in a domestic partnership
employment_status: Unemployed and currently looking for work
practice_description: Following a traumatic brain injury in 2014,  I turned to creating art to promote my physical and emotional healing. I began a practice of daily drawing and painting to build upon the neuro-optometric exercises that were prescribed by my doctors. Creating artwork is both a therapeutic and meditation practice for me.  I began creating works of art during my recovery for loved ones and began  posting the work on the internet using social media to connect with the outside world.  Limited by being largely homebound and taking prescribed meditation breaks throughout the day, I found tutors to teach me photoshop and watercolor at home. I combine daily journaling and meditation to develop sketches and calligraphy with ink, and pencil. I build upon select sketches for larger pieces using mixed media such as pastels and watercolors. My art has been described as happy, detailed and colorful. Artistic influences include traditional Japanese edo painting, fauve color, bauhaus design, expressionism, tiffany glass and a lifelong obsession miniature figurines and tiniest geometric details nature.
unique_resources: A personal collection of exhibition catalogs and art books acquired over a lifetime. Public access to the Teacher Resource Center at The Philadelphia Museum of Art. Professional and personal knowledge of the collections at both The Barnes and The PMA.  An online group of artists that support one another with questions about digital techniques, art licensing, copyrights and contracts. A remote art licensing agent who advises me on subject matter to pursue and medium to learn. A variety of dry and wet medium, a scanner, a printer and a home studio. An intermediate certification in COPIC makers for illustration.
